findings.title: Critical Need to Migrate from Deprecated Kafka Version
findings.description: The core message broker, running on Kafka 2.8, is reaching end-of-life in Q1 2026 and has known vulnerabilities. Customers are experiencing increased instability, which is critical for their operations. Immediate migration to Kafka 3.7+ is necessary to ensure platform stability and security.
assumptions: Customers rely heavily on Kafka for message brokering.
The migration will require significant engineering resources.

evidence.excerpt: Core message broker runs Kafka 2.8 which reaches end-of-life in Q1 2026.
We've noticed increasing instability in the Kafka layer — consumer rebalances are taking 10x longer than a year ago.
Risk Level: Critical; Impact: Core message broker for entire platform.

recommendations: Prioritize migration planning and resource allocation for Kafka upgrade.

findings.title: Vendor Lock-in Risk with Proprietary ETL SDK
findings.description: The reliance on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK poses a financial risk due to an impending price increase. Customers are concerned about the sustainability of their current setup and are seeking alternatives to avoid vendor lock-in.
assumptions: Customers are actively looking for alternatives to avoid high costs.
Migration to new solutions will require significant effort.

evidence.excerpt: VendorX was acquired last quarter and the new parent company announced a 3x price increase effective January 2026.
If the price triples, we'll need to migrate regardless.
Alternatives Evaluated: Apache Beam, dbt, Custom Flink.

recommendations: Evaluate open-source alternatives and develop a migration strategy.

findings.title: Security Concerns with Python 3.9 EOL
findings.description: The impending end-of-life for Python 3.9 raises security concerns as it will no longer receive patches. This violates vendor security policies for customers, necessitating an urgent upgrade to Python 3.12.
assumptions: Customers require ongoing security support for their software dependencies.

evidence.excerpt: Pipeline worker services run Python 3.9 which reaches end-of-life October 2025.
Python 3.9 EOL means no more security patches, which violates their vendor security policy.

recommendations: Plan and execute the migration to Python 3.12 as soon as possible.

findings.title: Need for Multi-Region Caching Solution
findings.description: The current single-region Redis cluster leads to high latency for customers outside the US. Migrating to a multi-region caching solution is essential to improve performance and reduce latency for global users.
assumptions: Customers in EU and APAC regions are experiencing significant latency issues.

evidence.excerpt: Customers in EU and APAC regions experience 200ms+ cache miss latency.

recommendations: Explore multi-region Redis solutions or alternative caching technologies.

summary: The analysis highlights critical migration needs due to deprecated technologies and vendor lock-in risks. Customers express urgent concerns regarding security and performance, necessitating immediate action to address these dependencies and improve the platform's reliability.
